title: Senecio inexpectatus
intro: Senecio inexpectatus, also known as Inexpectatus ragwort, is a species of plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve cognitive function, and boost the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Senecio inexpectatus include sesquiterpene lactones, such as inexpectatin and senecionine, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to treat various health conditions and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Senecio inexpectatus is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family Asteraceae. It is native to the Andes mountains in South America. The classification of Senecio inexpectatus includes its kingdom as Plantae and its classification within the plant kingdom as Eukaryota. At the rank of order, it is classified as Asterales. Further classification includes its family as Asteraceae and its genus as Senecio.
